perturbation.original_text: SRP will provide race day uniforms for up to a maximum of 12 personnel and a driver suit for the Driver. Consistent with the NASCAR rules and regulations, and excluding any patches required by NASCAR, Logan's shall have the exclusive right to promote its Logan's logo on uniforms and Driver's suit on the areas depicted on Exhibit A to this Agreement. Logan's also shall have the exclusive right to promote its Logan's trademark on the side of the helmet, as shown on Exhibit B to this Agreement. Uniforms and Driver's suit may display one patch each for up to two secondary or associate sponsors, provided such secondary or associate sponsors have been approved in advance by Logan's, such patches to be located as mutually agreed upon by Logan's and SRP. The Driver shall not appear in a race uniform or driving suit other than the Logan's uniform/suit in connection with or portraying involvement in NASCAR LMSC racing, whether or not used in product advertising or promotion.
perturbation.changed_text: SRP will provide race day uniforms for up to a maximum of 12 personnel and a driver suit for the Driver. Consistent with the NASCAR rules and regulations, and excluding any patches required by NASCAR, Logan's shall have the exclusive right to promote its Logan's logo on uniforms and Driver's suit. Uniforms and Driver's suit may display one patch each for up to two secondary or associate sponsors, provided such secondary or associate sponsors have been approved in advance by Logan's, such patches to be located as mutually agreed upon by Logan's and SRP. The Driver shall not appear in a race uniform or driving suit other than the Logan's uniform/suit in connection with or portraying involvement in NASCAR LMSC racing, whether or not used in product advertising or promotion.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the phrase 'on the areas depicted on Exhibit A to this Agreement. Logan's also shall have the exclusive right to promote its Logan's trademark on the side of the helmet, as shown on Exhibit B to this Agreement', the clause loses specificity about where Logan's logo can be displayed. While it retains Logan's exclusive rights, the removal of Exhibits A and B as a reference, creates an ambiguity in enforcement. Without these Exhibits, there are no defined areas, so the previous definition of the location of the logos becomes less clear, creating uncertainty in enforcement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: SRP will provide race day uniforms for up to a maximum of 12 personnel and a driver suit for the Driver. Consistent with the NASCAR rules and regulations, and excluding any patches required by NASCAR, Logan's shall have the exclusive right to promote its Logan's logo on uniforms and Driver's suit. Uniforms and Driver's suit may display one patch each for up to two secondary or associate sponsors, provided such secondary or associate sponsors have been approved in advance by Logan's, such patches to be located as mutually agreed upon by Logan's and SRP. The Driver shall not appear in a race uniform or driving suit other than the Logan's uniform/suit in connection with or portraying involvement in NASCAR LMSC racing, whether or not used in product advertising or promotion.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes specific exhibits (A and B) that previously defined the location of Logan's logos. This removal introduces ambiguity as the exclusive right to promote the logo is retained, but the defined areas are no longer specified. This ambiguity directly contradicts the implicit understanding within the original text (now also the contradicted text) which assumes these areas are defined, creating an inconsistency and potentially impacting enforcement. This fits the definition of 'Omissions – In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: In the event Logan's terminates this Agreement pursuant to this Article 10, Logan's shall be entitled to a pro rata refund of amounts prepaid for the current calendar year, with no further obligations under this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: In the event Logan's terminates this Agreement pursuant to this Article 10, Logan's shall be entitled to a refund of amounts prepaid for the current calendar year, with no further obligations under this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting the word 'pro rata', the calculation and the agreement on HOW the refund is supposed to be, becomes unclear and creates a contradiction, because there could be different interpretations on how the refund is supposed to be.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In the event Logan's terminates this Agreement pursuant to this Article 10, Logan's shall be entitled to a refund of amounts prepaid for the current calendar year, with no further obligations under this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies a 'pro rata refund', providing clarity on the refund calculation method. By omitting 'pro rata' in the changed text, the calculation method becomes ambiguous, directly contradicting the implied understanding of a specific calculation. The contradicted text section is also affected since it also now creates a contradiction in the document. Therefore, the omission introduces a strong in-text contradiction based on the definition: Omissions occur when essential language is removed or altered in one section, creating a contradiction with obligations or constraints stated in other sections.
